cot_prefix: Okay, let's tackle this question. The user wants to know what we can infer about garage sales based on the provided text. The question is multiple-choice with options A to D. First, I need to recall the relevant section of the text. The passage mentions that weekend outdoor garage sales have become part of American culture. It says they are usually held in spring and fall when the weather is good. The homeowner is clearing out accumulated items they no longer need, and the goods are in good condition. People can find great buys if they have time and patience. The items include various things like magazines, books, clothing, appliances, etc. Also, if the owner is moving, they might sell items far below their true worth. Looking at the options: A) Only held in spring. The text says "usually in spring and fall," so A is incorrect because it's not exclusive to spring. B) Goods are totally useless. The text states the items are no longer useful to the owner but are in good condition. So B is false. C) Popular with Americans. The text says garage sales are very much a part of American culture, implying popularity. So C is correct. D) All goods are sold below real value.
